experiment.name: CTA Button Copy Test - New Products Only
experiment.description: A/B test comparing two CTA button texts on new product pages (P007-P010)

A.name: Buy Now (Control)
A.description: Direct purchase CTA - standard e-commerce approach

B.name: Reserve 24h (Urgency)
B.description: Time-limited reservation CTA - creates urgency for 1-of-1 products

targeting.comment: Only applies to NEW products. Legacy products (P001-P006) keep existing CTAs.

distribution.comment: Users see consistent variant for 30 days via cookie

events.name: cta_view
events.description: CTA button was rendered on product page

events.name: add_to_cart
events.description: User added product to cart after clicking CTA

hypothesis.assumption: For 1-of-1 handcrafted products, time-limited 'Reserve' language will increase urgency and conversion rate compared to standard 'Buy Now' CTA
hypothesis.success_criteria: Variant B achieves >10% higher conversion rate than Variant A with statistical significance (p<0.05)

notes: This experiment ONLY applies to new product pages
Legacy products (P001-P006) keep their existing CTAs unchanged
Users are assigned to variant randomly on first view
Variant assignment is stored in cookie for 30 days (sticky sessions)
UTM session tracking integrates with this experiment to track traffic sources
Results should be reviewed weekly during active experiment period
